task_completion.description: Construct a cube of side length 2 with vertices at (0,0,0) and (2,2,2)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D cube is visible in the GeoGebra 3D Graphics view. The axes should show the cube spanning from 0 to 2 on the x, y, and z axes. The Algebra view may list the cube or its vertices.

task_completion.description: Create a plane passing through (1,1,1) and perpendicular to the space diagonal
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plane is visible intersecting the cube diagonally. The Algebra view may show the plane equation (x + y + z = 3) or points used to define it.

task_completion.description: Generate the intersection polygon representing the hexagonal cross-section
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct regular hexagon is visible where the plane intersects the cube. The Algebra view should list a polygon object with 6 vertices.

task_completion.description: Display the area of the cross-section using a text annotation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text label or measurement annotation is visible in the GeoGebra Graphics view showing the area of the hexagon (approximately 5.196 or 5.2).

A save dialog, file manager, or terminal window shows the file 'cube_hexagon.ggb' successfully saved in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory, or the GeoGebra window title reflects the correct filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the 3D geometry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual GeoGebra application interface with the 3D Graphics and Algebra views active, rather than a script or text editor generating a file blindly.

integrity.description: The area annotation is a genuine measurement or text object within the GeoGebra construction
integrity.visual_evidence: The text annotation showing the area is visible within the GeoGebra workspace alongside the 3D construction, not artificially overlaid or typed into an unrelated text editor.

integrity.description: The file was saved using the application's save functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is visible in the screenshots, showing interaction with the file system to place the .ggb file in the correct folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected area of the hexagonal cross-section is approximately 5.196 (accept values between 5.096 and 5.296 due to the 0.1 tolerance). The cube must have a side length of 2, spanning from (0,0,0) to (2,2,2). If visible in the Algebra view, the intersecting plane's equation should be x + y + z = 3. The final file must be named 'cube_hexagon.ggb'.
